discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape led by mega-cap tech and semis (SMH +2%, GOOGL +3.9%, NVDA +2.3%) on Trump China summit optimism, while financials and utilities lag on hotter-than-expected PPI (+6% YoY). Defense names (KTOS, AVAV) selling off sharply despite Anduril valuation news and Golden Dome tailwinds — notable divergence worth examining.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.0% and QQQ +1.06% on China summit/Nvidia delegation optimism
XLF -1.14% and KRE -1.6% despite Warsh Fed confirmation — hot PPI weighing on rate-cut hopes
XLU -1.15% and XLRE -0.83% on yield backup from PPI surprise
Defense (ITA only +0.14%) muted despite Anduril $60B valuation headline; mid-cap defense names selling off

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap defense name down hard with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings, while sector tape is flat-to-positive
discoveries.thesis: No company-specific catalyst visible. Possible explanations: (1) competitive read-through from Anduril's $60B private valuation raise signaling well-funded competition for drone/autonomous defense contracts, (2) profit-taking after extended run, (3) specific contract loss not yet in 8-K. -14.7% five-day drop suggests something cumulative is going on, but I can't articulate a directional bull/bear thesis at conf 3+ without knowing the driver.
discoveries.what_confirms: An 8-K in next 1-2 days disclosing a lost contract, or analyst notes citing Anduril competitive threat to Kratos drone programs
discoveries.what_kills: If price recovers on no news within days, it's likely just position unwind in a crowded long
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a sector peer raises private capital at a huge valuation step-up (Anduril here), public competitors can sell off on competitive-funding fears even without direct news — worth tracking systematically
discoveries.catalyst: No SEC filing or news found; possible Anduril competitive read-through
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Anduril doubled valuation to $60B+ working on Golden Dome interceptors — directly competes with Kratos drone/autonomy products
research_pointers: Check for any Anduril contract wins announced today that would displace Kratos
Review KTOS short interest and recent options flow
Look at AVAV co-movement (-4.66%) — same factor likely at work
Check if any sell-side downgrades hit pre-market

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Drone defense peer of KTOS selling off in sympathy/sector with no 8-K catalyst
discoveries.thesis: Co-moving with KTOS on what appears to be Anduril competitive concerns. Volume only 1.14x suggests this is sympathy, not company-specific news. Direction unclear without knowing the actual driver.
discoveries.what_confirms: If KTOS catalyst emerges as Anduril-related, AVAV reaction is rational sympathy
discoveries.what_kills: AVAV-specific bad news in an 8-K would change this from sympathy to fundamental
discoveries.catalyst: Likely sympathy with KTOS; Anduril valuation overhang
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No company-specific signals; co-movement with KTOS in same industry
research_pointers: Compare AVAV/KTOS price action over past 5 days — is this a paired trade unwind?
Check Anduril's product overlap with AVAV's Switchblade loitering munitions

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Application software name down hard on BELOW-average volume with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: Sub-1x volume on a -6.9% move is unusual — suggests a thin tape selloff rather than institutional distribution, possibly from a specific seller or a quiet downgrade. Five-day -8.9% indicates this is continuation of a slide, not a single-day event. Without identifying the driver I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: A sell-side downgrade note or competitor (e.g., Blue Yonder, SAP) supply-chain software win announcement would explain the pattern
discoveries.what_kills: If volume picks up to 2x+ on next session with a fundamental catalyst, the low-volume read is wrong
discoveries.what_to_learn: Large moves on sub-average volume often signal absence of buyers more than presence of sellers — different dynamic than capitulation, often slower to mean-revert
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — no SEC filings or news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; low volume on big move
research_pointers: Check for any analyst downgrades on MANH in the last 48 hours
Review whether any large supply-chain software customer announced an in-house build or competitor switch
Look at peer DSGX, SPS Commerce for co-movement

discoveries.name: Repligen Corporation
discoveries.setup: Bioprocessing tools name down sharply with -14.9% five-day drop, no 8-K, no scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: Cumulative two-week breakdown suggests something fundamental shifting in bioprocessing/biotech tools demand, but no specific catalyst visible in feeds. Peer Danaher/Sartorius commentary or a guide-down rumor could be driving this. Can't articulate direction at conf 3+ without source identification.
discoveries.what_confirms: A peer warning on bioprocessing demand, or a hospital/biopharma capex pullback data point
discoveries.what_kills: Recovery on no news suggests just rotation out of healthcare tools
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — possible peer read-through on bioprocessing demand
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No SEC or news catalysts found; sustained 5-day decline implies ongoing fundamental concern
research_pointers: Check Sartorius, Danaher Life Sciences commentary in last week
Review whether any biotech customer announced capex cuts
Look at XBI vs RGEN — XBI +0.7% today, RGEN -5.8%, so not biotech-broad

discoveries.name: Ducommun Incorporated
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction with 2.02 8-K and 2x volume on a small-cap aerospace supplier
discoveries.thesis: Move magnitude is consistent with an earnings beat on a $2.3B aerospace/defense supplier. 2x volume and +4.2% in a flat-to-down defense tape suggests company-specific beat rather than sector lift. Without seeing the actual print details I can't say whether it's overdone or underdone — but the magnitude is in the normal range for an earnings beat at this size.
discoveries.what_confirms: If the 8-K exhibits show meaningful guide-up or margin expansion, move may be underdone
discoveries.what_kills: If the print was an in-line beat with no guidance raise, +4% is the full reaction
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K filed yesterday with item 2.02 (results of operations); volume and direction consistent with positive earnings reaction
research_pointers: Pull the 8-K exhibit for actual Q1 numbers vs consensus
Check Ducommun's defense vs commercial aerospace mix — Boeing exposure relevant given Trump China/Boeing aircraft purchase chatter

discoveries.name: BridgeBio Pharma, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Biotech moving on 8-K cluster (earnings 5/7, material agreement 5/8, other event 5/13)
discoveries.thesis: The 5/13 8-K (item 8.01 'other events') filed today is the proximate driver — typically used for clinical data updates, label expansions, or partnership disclosures. Combined with the 5/8 material agreement filing (item 1.01 + 1.02), there's a sequence of business development activity. +4% on average volume is a measured reaction — neither overdone nor underdone without knowing the specific announcement content.
discoveries.what_confirms: If the 8.01 disclosure is a positive Acoramidis (ATTR-CM) data update or partnership expansion, the move could continue
discoveries.what_kills: If it's just an investor-day presentation re-filing, +4% is the full reaction
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Today's 8.01 filing came in tandem with earlier 5/8 material agreement activity; sequence suggests substantive corporate news
research_pointers: Read the 5/13 8-K exhibit to identify the 'other event' — likely Acoramidis-related
Review the 5/8 material agreement vs termination — could be licensing deal restructure
Check ATTR-CM competitive landscape (Pfizer tafamidis, Alnylam)

discoveries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
discoveries.setup: Post-earnings drift in met coal name following 5/8 earnings (-9% over 5 days)
discoveries.thesis: Continued post-earnings selling after the 5/8 print (item 2.02) and Reg FD disclosure. Met coal pricing remains under pressure and the cumulative -9% five-day move plus today's -4.6% is consistent with a soft print followed by analyst cuts. Magnitude appropriate for a commodity producer in a weak pricing environment.
discoveries.what_confirms: Met coal benchmark prices continuing to decline; further sell-side cuts to 2026 EBITDA
discoveries.what_kills: China stimulus announcement during Trump-Xi summit lifting steel/met coal expectations could sharply reverse this
discoveries.what_to_learn: Trump-Xi summit creates two-way risk in China-exposed commodities — met coal and steel feedstock names are non-obvious China policy beneficiaries worth watching
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Earnings 8-K from 5/8 with item 2.02; subsequent 5-day decline of -9% shows post-earnings drift consistent with a soft print
research_pointers: Check Australian premium hard coking coal spot benchmark trend
Review whether any sell-side has cut AMR estimates this week
Consider China steel policy as a potential AMR catalyst this week

discoveries.name: Axsome Therapeutics, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Mid-cap biotech +5% on BELOW-average volume with no 8-K, no scheduled earnings, in a flattish XBI
discoveries.thesis: Sub-1x volume on a +5% move suggests short-covering or thin tape rather than institutional accumulation. With no visible catalyst and XBI only +0.7%, this is hard to attribute. Could be Auvelity script-data positive read or pipeline rumor, but I can't construct a conf 3+ directional case.
discoveries.what_confirms: Weekly script data showing Auvelity acceleration, or AXS-05 / AXS-14 readout proximity
discoveries.what_kills: Reversal on next session with no news suggests technical-only move
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals; low volume on positive move
research_pointers: Check IQVIA weekly script data for Auvelity
Review upcoming AXSM catalyst calendar (Symbravo launch metrics)
Check short interest — high SI + low-volume rip often precedes squeeze

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Anduril doubles private valuation to $60B+ working on Golden Dome interceptors
Trump-Xi summit in Beijing with CEO delegation including Boeing's Ortberg; traders pricing in tariff truce extension and Boeing aircraft purchases

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump confirms CEO delegation to China including Jensen Huang (NVDA), Tim Cook (AAPL), Musk (TSLA), Boeing's Ortberg, Goldman's Solomon, Citi's Fraser, Micron's Mehrotra, Qualcomm's Amon. States he will ask Xi to 'open up' China.
trump_signals.note: Risk-on signal for semi/mega-cap tech with China revenue exposure — already largely priced today (NVDA +2.3%, SMH +2%, GOOGL +3.9%). The marginal opportunity is in second-derivative names: aerospace suppliers (DCO, HEI, TDG) if Boeing announces orders, and China commodity proxies. Watch for actual deliverables from the summit — tariff truce extension is largely priced; new market access concessions are not.

ai_announcements.headline: Amazon launches Alexa for Shopping AI assistant in search bar
ai_announcements.summary: Amazon's Alexa+ powered shopping assistant provides personalized recommendations and automates shopping across Amazon and other online retailers via voice/touch.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic courts small business owners; surpasses OpenAI in business customer share per Ramp
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic launching SMB-focused offering and reportedly leading OpenAI in paid business customers (34.4% vs 32.3% on Ramp data). Potential headwind to SMB-focused software vendors and to OpenAI partners.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Manhattan Associates sells supply chain/WMS/order management to enterprise customers — Anthropic SMB push and Amazon's shopping AI don't directly threaten their core. MANH could integrate LLMs into their suite as a tailwind. Sticky enterprise deployments.
research_pointers: Check if MANH had company-specific news today (analyst downgrade, guidance)
Compare to peer SPS/DSGX moves — is this sector-wide AI fear or idiosyncratic?
5-day -8.88% suggests ongoing pressure beyond today's headlines
Note: Anthropic-related call flagged for bias grading

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most stock moves in market_data appear unrelated to today's AI announcements. KTOS/AVAV/DCO are defense names — no AI catalyst in feed. RGEN, AXSM, BBIO are healthcare/biotech with their own catalysts (BBIO has 8-Ks). AMR is coal with earnings 8-K. Only MANH has a plausible (weak) AI-displacement narrative worth flagging, and even that is a stretch given low product overlap.
